buildNote: Aggregate indicator manifest for the Policy Preferences Lab data explorer. Public chart options have been cleaned to remove sample-size, respondent-count, and demographic-only measures, leaving substantive outcomes, beliefs, treatment effects, incidence, and policy preference indicators. + American exceptionalism (JEBO 2021) microdata-derived aggregates from the HoyMager_replication_final package. Audit prune: removed single-row pooled bars, background-context views, silently-truncated rankings and the duplicate 3a-3b summary view.

sources.title: Attitudes towards reducing fossil fuel subsidies: Evidence across 12 middle-income countries
sources.notes: Aggregates derived from Data/Clean/allrawdata_weight.dta in the uploaded RR_MIC_2024_85-v03 reproducibility package. Weighted means use wt_rake2t, as specified by the package do-file.

sources.title: How does progressivity impact tax morale? Experimental evidence across developing countries
sources.sourceType: Uploaded study datasets and Stata graph outputs
sources.notes: Microdata and figure-derived aggregate indicators from uploaded tax morale study files: surveydata.dta, figure1.dta, figure2.dta, figurea1.dta and original Stata graph files for Figure 3. The public explorer reports aggregate indicators only, not respondent-level data.

sources.title: Public preferences for economic reforms are shaped more by design than cost
sources.status: Table-derived country aggregates + respondent-level expert survey aggregates loaded
sources.notes: Country survey microdata folders are empty in the uploaded RR_WLD_2025_426 package; table-derived country indicators remain loaded, and respondent-level aggregates are now derived from the raw expert survey CSV using the package cleaning do-file.

sources.title: Why are relatively poor people not more supportive of redistribution? Evidence from a randomized survey experiment across ten countries
sources.notes: Aggregates derived from openICPSR 128261 input files crosscountry.dta, UK.dta and australia.dta, following the variable construction in the replication do-files. Public explorer reports aggregate indicators only.

sources.title: American exceptionalism? Differences in the elasticity of preferences for redistribution between the United States and Western Europe
sources.notes: Aggregates recomputed from input/US_NE_ES_final.dta, input/UK_final.dta and input/Denmark_final.dta in the HoyMager_replication_final package, applying the harmonisation and age–gender weights from programs/2_master.do.

sources.title: Horizontal equity of taxation: Citizen beliefs and policy preferences
sources.notes: Treatment-effect estimates recomputed from cleanforqvals.dta using the exact specifications in 4_figures.do (strata/country fixed effects, country-clustered SEs, 90% CIs). Includes both summary indices and their individual z-scored components, per the index construction in 2_cleaning.do. Effects are in standard-deviation units. This online-survey component covers five of the paper’s six countries.
